decks.notes: This Dimir Control deck focuses on disrupting the opponent's game plan through countering spells, removing threats, and controlling the board while also utilizing card advantage and library manipulation to gain an edge in card advantage and selection. The deck incorporates a mix of removal spells, counterspells, and library manipulation tools to control the pace of the game and ultimately outvalue the opponent. It aims to maintain control of the battlefield while slowly grinding out advantages to overwhelm the opponent and secure victory through efficient resource management and strategic decision-making.

This Boros Aggro deck focuses on quickly overwhelming the opponent with aggressive creatures and burn spells. The key strategy is to maintain board presence through efficient creatures like Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer and Guide of Souls, while also using removal spells like Galvanic Discharge and Goblin Bombardment to control the board. The deck also utilizes powerful planeswalkers like Ajani, Nacatl Pariah and impactful spells like Blood Moon to disrupt the opponent's game plan. By combining fast creatures, burn spells, and disruptive elements, this deck aims to quickly close out games before the opponent can stabilize.

This Boros Aggro deck focuses on using aggressive creatures like Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer and Guide of Souls to quickly overwhelm your opponent. It also includes powerful spells like Goblin Bombardment and Ajani, Nacatl Pariah to maintain pressure. The deck aims to take control of the game early on and keep the pressure on with efficient creatures and removal spells. Additionally, utilizing cards like Seasoned Pyromancer and Ranger-Captain of Eos to generate card advantage and ensure consistency. Playing this deck requires quick decision-making and strategic use of resources to maintain aggression while controlling the game tempo.

The deck focuses on using instant reanimation spells like Goryo's Vengeance to quickly bring powerful creatures like Atraxa, Grand Unifier and Griselbrand onto the battlefield. Quantum Riddler and Psychic Frog provide card draw and manipulation to help find combo pieces. Solitude and Ephemerate offer removal and protection. The goal is to assemble the combo pieces quickly and efficiently to overwhelm the opponent with powerful creatures and control elements. The deck aims to win through a combination of aggressive reanimation, disruptive control, and efficient card advantage.

This Dimir Control deck focuses on disrupting and controlling the opponent's game plan through a combination of counterspells, removal, and card advantage. It uses powerful control pieces like Wan Shi Tong, Librarian and Sheoldred's Edict to outvalue the opponent and win through attrition. The deck also includes various counter spells and removal spells like Fatal Push to handle threats efficiently. With a strong focus on card advantage and disruption, this deck aims to control the game and outmaneuver the opponent to secure victory.

The main concept of this deck is to ramp into powerful Eldrazi creatures using a combination of mana acceleration cards like Utopia Sprawl and Eldrazi Temple, as well as cards like Talisman of Impulse. The deck also includes control elements like Kozilek's Return and Malevolent Rumble to deal with threats, as well as cards like Sowing Mycospawn and Formidable Speaker to generate value. The goal is to overwhelm the opponent with massive creatures like Emrakul, the Promised End and World Breaker while disrupting their strategy with removal and control elements.

This deck aims to generate a high amount of mana quickly through the use of spells like Pyretic Ritual and Ruby Medallion, allowing for explosive turns where powerful spells can be cast for reduced costs. The deck includes cards like Ral, Monsoon Mage and Past in Flames to help recur spells from the graveyard, maintaining a constant stream of resources. With the ability to generate a large amount of mana and cast multiple spells in a turn, the deck aims to finish off opponents with a flurry of spells like Grapeshot and strike it down in one explosive turn.

This UW Control deck focuses on disrupting opponent's strategies through countermagic, removal, and card advantage provided by Narset and Teferi. It utilizes cards like Orim's Chant and Counterspell to control the opponent's spells, while Prismatic Ending and Solitude handle threats on the board. Monumental Henge provides card advantage and mana ramp, while Isochron Scepter can be used to repeat important spells. The deck also includes a mix of other utility cards like Day's Undoing and Consult the Star Charts to maintain card advantage and find key pieces to win the game.

The key theme of this deck is to quickly and aggressively build up a strong board presence with a mix of powerful creatures and spells. The deck focuses on utilizing efficient creatures like Scion of Draco and Territorial Kavu, along with burn spells like Lightning Bolt to control the board and deal damage to the opponent. The inclusion of Leyline Binding and Stubborn Denial helps protect your creatures and spells, while cards like The Legend of Roku and Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer provide additional card advantage and utility. Overall, the deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with early aggression and tempo plays.

This deck focuses on assembling a powerful combo by utilizing Devoted Druid and Vizier of Remedies to generate infinite mana, allowing you to search for key creatures like Craterhoof Behemoth with Green Sun's Zenith. Leyline of Abundance helps accelerate your mana production, while Birds of Paradise and Noble Hierarch provide additional ramp. Agatha's Soul Cauldron can be used to recur key creatures from the graveyard. The deck also includes a toolbox of creatures to deal with various situations, making it a versatile and powerful combo deck in the Modern format.

The main focus of this deck is to quickly ramp up mana using cards like Ruby Medallion and Pyretic Ritual, while using Ral, Monsoon Mage and other spells to generate storm count. By casting multiple spells in a turn, the deck aims to eventually cast Grapeshot with a high storm count to deal lethal damage to the opponent. The deck also utilizes Wish to tutor for specific cards needed for the combo. Overall, the deck's strategy is to efficiently and explosively combo off to win the game in a single turn.

This Boros Aggro deck focuses on quickly overwhelming the opponent with a combination of aggressive creatures like Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer and powerful spells like Galvanic Discharge and Lightning Bolt. The deck also includes cards like Ajani, Nacatl Pariah and Voice of Victory to boost the team's strength and provide additional advantages. With a mix of creature power and burn spells, this deck aims to win the game swiftly before the opponent can set up their own strategy. The inclusion of cards like Arena of Glory and Goblin Bombardment adds versatility and resilience to the deck.

The deck aims to quickly cycle through cards and disrupt the opponent's strategy until it can cast Living End, which will then bring back all the creatures from both players' graveyards onto the battlefield. The deck utilizes cards like Street Wraith and Subtlety to cycle through the deck efficiently, while also running disruption spells like Force of Negation and Sink into Stupor to control the game. The creatures in the deck are primarily flying and have additional abilities to provide advantage once they hit the battlefield, creating a powerful combo to overwhelm the opponent.

This deck focuses on generating mana quickly and efficiently through cards like Manamorphose, Pyretic Ritual, and Ruby Medallion to cast powerful spells and combo off. The key strategy is to use Ral, Monsoon Mage to draw cards and deal damage, while also utilizing Past in Flames to flashback key spells. The deck aims to win the game by chaining together spells with high synergy, such as Flame of Anor and Desperate Ritual, to overwhelm the opponent and secure victory. The inclusion of Wish allows for additional flexibility and access to key sideboard cards.

This deck focuses on generating red mana quickly and efficiently to cast powerful storm spells like Grapeshot. It utilizes cards like Pyretic Ritual and Desperate Ritual to ramp up mana production, while also using cards like Reckless Impulse and Wish to dig for combo pieces. Ral, Monsoon Mage is key in providing card advantage and further fueling the storm count. The goal is to quickly assemble the necessary pieces to chain spells together and overwhelm the opponent with a flurry of spell copies and direct damage.

The main idea behind this deck is to control the game by managing your opponent's resources and disrupting their strategy. The deck focuses on removal spells like Path to Exile and Wrath of God, as well as cards like Ghost Quarter and Field of Ruin to disrupt your opponent's lands. It also includes card draw and utility cards like Mazemind Tome and Karn, the Great Creator to help you stay ahead. With efficient removal and control elements, this deck aims to outlast and outmaneuver your opponent to secure victory.
